Motion To Compel State Compensation Insurance Fund To Produce Additional Documents In Response To Pltf'S Third Request For Production Of Documents;
Off Calendar Nov-01-2006 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearance:
Sascha Henry, Esq., for State Compensation Insurance Fund, Defendant/Opposing Party
Marina N. Vitek, Esq., for Nusake, Inc., Plaintiff/Moving Party

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Plaintif Nushake, Inc.'s Motion to Compel Production of Additional Documents In Response to Plaintiff's Third Request for Production of Documents is denied as the requests are overly broad.
Plaintiff's separate statement identifies the issues as being cancellation based upon an alleged "split risk", the failure to comply with an audit, the failure to report payroll, and the failure to pay premiums. Yet each request seeks the entirety of each specified manual. That is overly broad. Reference to a manual in a deposition does not make the entire manual relevant. There is no support for the argument that there have been inconsistent interpretations of relevance which should result in an evidentiary sanction compelling production of documents without a determination of relevance. COMM: EVERETT A. HEWLETT

Mtn For Directions Re Certain Docs Produced By Pltf
Off Calendar Mar-24-2006 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearance:
Sascha Henry, appeared for Defendant/Moving party
Marina Vitek, appeared via telephone for Plaintiff/Opposing party

Ruling:
Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Pursuant to Defendant/Cross-Complainant State Compensation Insurance Fund's Motion For Directions Re: Certain Documents Produced By Plaintiffs, the court finds that the documents that are the subject of this motion were inadvertently produced. The court finds that there has been no conduct which evidences an intent to waive the privilege, and that the disclosure and representations made regarding the existence of privileged documents were accidental. In State Compensation Insurance Fund v WPS, Inc., 70 Cal.App.4th 644, the court refused to adopt a finding and make an order that waiver occurs by reason of a slip-up.
There is no need for a privilege log as Defendant knows the author and recipient of the documents and the basis for the claim of privilege. As a requirement of a privilege log is to inform a requesting party as to the basis for the claim of privilege, such a log would be redundant; and it is not required in this case as to those documents. The disclosure of one of the documents to Mainstay was not a waiver of the privilege.
The court orders the documents returned and that no reference be made to the existence of the documents.
Defendant's objection to evidence is overruled. The statement is not offered for the truth of the matter asserted. COMM: EVERETT A. HEWLETT
